headline: The U.S. government ordered Anthropic to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5 offline worldwide to block foreign-national access, even as a critical Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day drained gigabytes from hundreds of organizations.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Governance
emerging_trends.text: Governments and vendors moved hard against AI misuse in a single day, with Washington pulling Anthropic's top models offline and Google suing a network that weaponized Gemini for phishing.

emerging_trends.subject: Linux Backdoors
emerging_trends.text: Attackers and spies converged on Linux, hijacking more than 400 Arch packages and burying a decade-long Velvet Ant implant inside PAM and OpenSSH.

emerging_trends.subject: Breach Penalties
emerging_trends.text: Regulators and courts kept raising the price of lost data, from South Korea's record 409 million dollar Coupang fine to a 47 million dollar 23andMe settlement.

emerging_trends.subject: Enterprise Zero-Days
emerging_trends.text: Long-lived flaws in widely deployed software bit hard, as a critical Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day fed mass theft and phpBB patched an auth bypass that lurked for ten years.

topics.title: U.S. Forces Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5
topics.last_24h: Anthropic disabled Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for every user worldwide after a federal order arrived at 5:21 p.m. ET demanding it block foreign nationals.
topics.summary: The Trump administration directed Anthropic to bar all foreign nationals, inside the country or out, from its two most capable models, citing national security and a jailbreak. Anthropic complied and suspended both models globally, though it disputes the rationale, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the capability common elsewhere. The move marks an aggressive use of export controls against a frontier AI vendor and strands paying customers overnight.

sources.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls
U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals

topics.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day Drives Mass Theft
topics.last_24h: Researchers detailed an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day that ShinyHunters exploited to steal gigabytes from hundreds of organizations, hitting American universities hardest.
topics.summary: A critical flaw in Oracle's PeopleSoft ERP, widely deployed across higher education, let attackers reach data without authenticating. The ShinyHunters extortion crew capitalized, exfiltrating large troves from universities and other enterprises. Administrators running PeopleSoft should patch immediately and hunt for signs of bulk data theft.

sources.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed

topics.title: Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked
topics.last_24h: Attackers hijacked more than 400 Arch User Repository packages, rewriting build scripts to drop a Rust credential stealer and an eBPF rootkit.
topics.summary: The Arch User Repository, a community collection separate from Arch Linux's official packages, builds software from user-supplied scripts. Attackers seized over 400 of them and inserted malware that harvests developer secrets and access tokens; with root it loads an eBPF rootkit to hide. Anyone who built an AUR package this week should rotate credentials and inspect for the rootkit.

sources.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer

topics.title: Velvet Ant Backdoors Linux Login for a Decade
topics.last_24h: Sygnia reported that China-nexus group Velvet Ant backdoored Linux PAM and OpenSSH login components and hid inside a target network for nearly a decade.
topics.summary: Velvet Ant planted its access in the software that authenticates logins, PAM and OpenSSH, where routine cleanup never reached. The group held persistence on the victim network for close to ten years. Defenders should audit authentication binaries and modules, not only the endpoints they watch most closely.

sources.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade

topics.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Over Gemini Abuse
topics.last_24h: Google sued a Chinese cybercrime network it accuses of weaponizing its Gemini AI to run a phishing-as-a-service kit called Outsider against Americans.
topics.summary: Google alleges the network built and ran the Outsider phishing-as-a-service kit, using its Gemini agent to craft and send smishing texts to U.S. targets. The lawsuit escalates how vendors police abuse of their own AI tools, taking the fight to the courts. It feeds the day's broader anxiety over AI in criminal hands.

sources.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing

topics.title: Conti Affiliate Pleads Guilty in U.S. Court
topics.last_24h: A Ukrainian national whom the United States extradited from Ireland pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation.
topics.summary: The defendant, whom the U.S. extradited last year, admitted his role in Conti, among the most prolific ransomware crews before its 2022 collapse. The plea continues a slow string of arrests and convictions against the gang's affiliates. It signals that extradition and prosecution stay live threats years after a crew disbands.

sources.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation

topics.title: Record Breach Penalties Hit Coupang and 23andMe
topics.last_24h: South Korea fined Coupang a record 409 million dollars over a data breach, and a bankruptcy administrator approved a 47 million dollar settlement for 23andMe victims.
topics.summary: South Korea's commission levied its largest-ever data-breach penalty on the retailer Coupang, eclipsing the 88.8 million dollar fine against SK Telecom earlier this year. Separately, roughly 7 million 23andMe customers whose genetic data thieves stole beginning in April 2023 will share a 47 million dollar fund. Both cases show the mounting financial cost of failing to protect personal data.

sources.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims

topics.title: Section 702 Surveillance Authority Heads to Lapse
topics.last_24h: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act heads toward its first lapse since 2008 as Congress deadlocks over reauthorization.
topics.summary: Section 702 lets U.S. intelligence agencies collect the communications of foreign targets abroad, sweeping in some Americans' data along the way. Legislative gridlock has pushed the program toward its first expiration since lawmakers passed it in 2008. A lapse would force agencies to wind down a major collection authority and would reignite the long fight over privacy and surveillance.

sources.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock

Marlins try to extend road win streak in matchup against
2026 MLB ABS challenge system tracker: Team, player

business_politics.text: The United States and Iran have settled on a final agreed text for a ceasefire deal that Pakistan's prime minister says will be signed within 24 hours, reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Oil futures settled at multimonth lows and Treasury yields fell on the week as the conflict that briefly reignited days earlier moved toward resolution.

sources.title: Pakistan says US-Iran deal to be signed within 24 hours
Oil Futures Fall on Expectations of U.S.-Iran Agreement

business_politics.text: SpaceX raised $75 billion in the largest initial public offering on record, and its shares jumped nearly a fifth on debut to value the company at $2.1 trillion, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

sources.title: SpaceX, Now Worth $2.1 Trillion, Pulls Off Goldilocks Debut
SpaceX's surge on debut makes Musk world's first trillionaire

business_politics.text: US headline annual inflation reached 4.2%, the highest since April 2023, sharpening the dilemma for a Federal Reserve weighing whether to wait out renewed price pressures.

sources.title: Chart of the Week: US headline inflation matters to consumers

business.text: Allegheny County extended the public comment period on a proposal requiring every county business to provide 18 weeks of paid parental leave. Supporters call the mandate overdue; opponents, including the county EMS council, warn it could drive employers away.

sources.title: Allegheny County extends public comment period for paid parental leave proposal
Letter to the editor: Supporting working parents is critical, especially to EMS services

business.text: A Manor couple launched WanderBrew, a mobile coffee shop built inside a 1978 trailer that Kelly Dowdrick found on Facebook Marketplace 32 minutes after pitching the idea to her husband.

sources.title: Manor couple launches WanderBrew coffee trailer

business.text: Polymath Park in Westmoreland County opened a row of cedar, treehouse-inspired dining pods tucked among the trees, selling seclusion as the draw for outdoor diners.

sources.title: Treehouse-inspired pods offer nature dining in Polymath Park

around_town.text: Pennsylvania legislative leaders say they expect to pass a state budget on time, avoiding a repeat of last year's 135-day impasse that delayed billions in funding to schools, counties, and nonprofits.

sources.title: Pa. lawmakers optimistic about passing budget on time as deadline looms

around_town.text: Section 702, the federal warrantless-surveillance program aimed at foreign targets, lapsed overnight after Congress failed to pass an extension, raising questions about national-security coverage and how companies will field data requests.

sources.title: Section 702: Congress just let a surveillance tool expire, but what is it and what's next?

around_town.text: Drivers parked along Ward Street in Oakland found their cars towed this week with what residents call inadequate notice, though city officials say the signage had proper approval.

sources.title: Oakland residents say cars were towed without proper notice, officials say signage was approved

around_town.text: Educators across Western Pennsylvania are split over how much screen time belongs in elementary classrooms, weighing scrolling against the foundational work of reading, writing, and math.

sources.title: Debate surrounding elementary school screen time reverberates throughout Western Pa.

events.text: The Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival returns to Butler this weekend, marking the town as the Jeep's birthplace with a self-styled Jeep invasion.

sources.title: Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival marks Jeep's birthplace in Butler, Pa.

events.text: Pogopalooza, the world championships of pogo, runs through the weekend in Pittsburgh, opening with a high-jump competition downtown Friday before events continue in Wilkinsburg.

sources.title: Pogopalooza bounces back to Pittsburgh this weekend

events.text: Westmoreland County hosts a slate of weekend events, among them a Flag Day observance, an art show, and a fishing derby.

sources.title: Westmoreland happenings: Flag Day observance, art show, fishing derby, more

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era is ending as OpenAI and Anthropic file to go public, reading the IPOs as a scramble for exit liquidity by two firms that burn billions a year with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fable 5, Anthropic Alignment, AI Tiers
reading.summary: Thompson examines Fable 5, the public version of Mythos, judging it highly capable while warning that its release establishes troubling precedents around alignment and AI tiers.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn't AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport questions why AI has not eliminated jobs the way machines eliminated manual labor, testing the analogy AI company leaders draw between automation and their own technology.
